normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit precondition is asserted by the implementation. The only required property for correctness is that the input slice is sorted nondecreasing, but the code itself does not enforce it.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), (∀ i j : Fin arr.length, i.1 ≤ j.1 → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) → True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the array is sorted in nondecreasing order (monotone): for any indices i ≤ j, arr[i] ≤ arr[j].

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), (binarySearchSpec arr target) ↔ (∃ i : Fin arr.length, arr[i] = target)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the intended specification: the target is present iff there exists an index i within bounds such that arr[i] = target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (lo hi : Nat),
  lo ≤ hi ∧ hi ≤ arr.length →
  (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 < lo → arr[k] < target) ∧
  (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 ≥ hi → arr[k] ≥ target)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Maintain the standard binary-search invariant: all indices strictly below lo cannot contain the target (their values are < target), and all indices at or above hi cannot contain the target (their values are ≥ target).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  (∀ i j : Fin arr.length, i.1 ≤ j.1 → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) →
  let lo := 0; let hi := arr.length;
  lo ≤ hi ∧ hi ≤ arr.length ∧
  (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 < lo → arr[k] < target) ∧
  (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 ≥ hi → arr[k] ≥ target)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Show the invariant holds at loop start: lo = 0 and hi = arr.length. The left side is vacuous; the right side is vacuous because there are no indices ≥ arr.length.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (lo hi : Nat),
  (∀ i j : Fin arr.length, i.1 ≤ j.1 → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) →
  lo ≤ hi ∧ hi ≤ arr.length →
  (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 < lo → arr[k] < target) ∧
  (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 ≥ hi → arr[k] ≥ target) →
  (let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2;
   arr[mid] < target →
     let lo' := mid + 1; let hi' := hi;
     lo' ≤ hi' ∧ hi' ≤ arr.length ∧
     (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 < lo' → arr[k] < target) ∧
     (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 ≥ hi' → arr[k] ≥ target)) ∧
  (let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2;
   arr[mid] ≥ target →
     let lo' := lo; let hi' := mid;
     lo' ≤ hi' ∧ hi' ≤ arr.length ∧
     (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 < lo' → arr[k] < target) ∧
     (∀ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 ≥ hi' → arr[k] ≥ target))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the invariant is preserved across each branch of the loop. If arr[mid] < target, move lo to mid+1; otherwise move hi to mid. Sortedness is used to justify the value bounds for the discarded region.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  ∀ (lo hi : Nat), lo ≤ hi → hi ≤ arr.length →
  (let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2;
   (arr[mid] < target → lo' = mid + 1 ∧ hi' = hi ∧ hi' - lo' < hi - lo) ∧
   (arr[mid] ≥ target → lo' = lo ∧ hi' = mid ∧ hi' - lo' < hi - lo))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Show the loop variant (hi - lo) strictly decreases on each iteration, ensuring termination.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (i : Nat),
  i < arr.length →
  (binarySearchResult arr target = some i) →
  arr[i] = target
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some(i), then i is within bounds and arr[i] equals the target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  (binarySearchResult arr target = none) →
  ¬ (∃ i : Fin arr.length, arr[i] = target)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns None, then there is no index i in bounds with arr[i] = target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  (∀ i j : Fin arr.length, i.1 ≤ j.1 → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) →
  (∃ i : Fin arr.length, arr[i] = target) →
  (binarySearchResult arr target ≠ none)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assuming sortedness, if the target exists in the array, the algorithm must eventually return Some(i) for some matching index (any matching index is acceptable).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (res : Option Nat),
  binarySearchResult arr target = res →
  (res = none ∨ (∃ i : Nat, res = some i ∧ i < arr.length))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned index (when present) is always within array bounds.
